summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn continued the 'Jesus Over Giants' series with a message titled 'He Saves Me From the Storm,' based on Acts 27. He taught that storms and shipwrecks are an inevitable part of life, but Jesus is present and saves us through them. Pastor Travis emphasized that it's not a matter of 'if' but 'when' we face hardship, drawing from the story of Paul's shipwreck where God promised safety despite the vessel's destruction. He outlined five key responses for navigating life's storms: focusing on God's voice, remembering we are God's property, ensuring we are surrounded by godly people, cutting off anchors that hold us back, and holding on to God's provision in the brokenness. The message concluded with a powerful reminder that God's blessings and favor can emerge from our fractures and failures, and an altar call for salvation, reinforcing that our ultimate hope and identity are found in Jesus Christ.
mainPoints: Focus on God's voice to guide and comfort you through life's storms.
Remember your identity as God's property; nothing can separate you from His love.
Surround yourself with godly people and let go of anchors that hinder your spiritual progress.

versesMentioned.text: When it was decided that we would sail for Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were handed over to a centurion named Julius, who belonged to the Imperial Regiment.
We moved along the coast with difficulty and came to a place called Fair Havens, near the town of Lasea.

After they had gone a long time without food, Paul stood up before them and said: 'Men, you should have taken my advice not to sail from Crete; then you would have spared yourselves this damage and loss.'

But now I urge you to keep up your courage, because not one of you will be lost; only the ship will be destroyed. Last night an angel of the God to whom I belong and whom I serve stood beside me and said, 'Do not be afraid, Paul. You must stand trial before Caesar; and God has graciously given you the lives of all who sail with you.'
Nevertheless, we must run aground on some island.'
Cutting loose the anchors, they left them in the sea and at the same time untied the ropes that held the rudders. Then they hoisted the foresail to the wind and made for the beach.
In this way everyone reached land safely.
you were bought at a price. Therefore honor God with your bodies.

Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall trouble or hardship or persecution or famine or nakedness or danger or sword? ... No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us. For I am convinced that neither death nor life, neither angels nor demons, neither the present nor the future, nor any powers, neither height nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God that is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a great cloud of witnesses, let us throw off everything that hinders and the sin that so easily entangles. And let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us, fixing our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and perfecter of faith.

This is the word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD: 'Go down to the potter’s house, and there I will give you my message.' So I went down to the potter’s house, and I saw him working at the wheel. But the pot he was shaping from the clay was marred in his hands; so the potter formed it into another pot, shaping it as seemed best to him.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.
If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: Acts 27:22-24 (NIV) - 'But now I urge you to keep up your courage... God has graciously given you the lives of all who sail with you.'
Monday.action: Identify one current worry or 'storm' and write down a prayer declaring God's presence and promise in it.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank You that You are with me in this situation. Help me to take courage today, knowing You are in control.

Tuesday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 6:20 (NIV) - 'you were bought at a price.'
Tuesday.action: Write down the statement 'I am God's property' and reflect on what that means for your worth and security.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, remind me today that I belong to You. Help me to reject any lie that contradicts my value as Your child.

Wednesday.scripture: Acts 27:40 (NIV) - 'Cutting loose the anchors, they left them in the sea...'
Wednesday.action: Prayerfully consider one 'anchor' (a habit, relationship, or thought pattern) holding you back and take one practical step to release it.
Wednesday.prayer: God, give me the courage to cut loose what is holding me back from moving forward with You. I trust You with the outcome.

Thursday.scripture: Hebrews 12:1 (NIV) - '...let us throw off everything that hinders and the sin that so easily entangles.'
Thursday.action: Do one thing today that aligns with your spiritual health, like a short walk with prayer or choosing encouraging media.
Thursday.prayer: Father, help me to run my race with perseverance today. Show me what I need to throw off to follow You more closely.

Friday.scripture: Acts 27:44 (NIV) - 'In this way everyone reached land safely.'
Friday.action: Look for one 'plank' or piece of 'debris'—a small blessing, person, or resource—God has provided in a difficult area of your life and thank Him for it.
Friday.prayer: Thank You, God, for providing for me even in broken situations. Help me to see and hold onto Your faithful provision.

Saturday.scripture: Jeremiah 18:4 (NIV) - '...so the potter formed it into another pot, shaping it as seemed best to him.'
Saturday.action: Spend 10 minutes in quiet reflection, surrendering an area of your life that feels 'marred' or broken to God, trusting Him to reshape it.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, I am the clay and You are the Potter. I surrender my broken pieces to You. Shape me into who You want me to be.
